title: Automate Anything with Cron
summary: Five real-world automation patterns for Hermes cron jobs: website change monitor with state tracking, weekly report generation, GitHub repository watcher, data collection pipeline with script+agent split, and multi-skill chained workflows.

cli_commands.purpose: Create a cron job from CLI
Create a cron job from chat
Trigger a job immediately for testing
Pause a job without deleting it

slash_commands.purpose: Edit a job's schedule or prompt

code_examples.caption: Website change monitor script
code_examples.code:
# ~/.hermes/scripts/watch-site.py
import hashlib, json, os, urllib.request

URL = "https://example.com/pricing"
STATE_FILE = os.path.expanduser("~/.hermes/scripts/.watch-site-state.json")

req = urllib.request.Request(URL, headers={"User-Agent": "Hermes-Monitor/1.0"})
content = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30).read().decode()
current_hash = hashlib.sha256(content.encode()).hexdigest()

# ... state management and diff detection

code_examples.caption: Create monitoring job with script
code_examples.code: /cron add "every 1h" "If the script output says CHANGE DETECTED, summarize what changed on the page and why it might matter. If it says NO_CHANGE, respond with just [SILENT]." --script ~/.hermes/scripts/watch-site.py --name "Pricing monitor" --deliver telegram

code_examples.caption: Weekly report
code_examples.code:
/cron add "0 9 * * 1" "Generate a weekly report covering:

1. Search the web for the top 5 AI news stories from the past week
2. Search GitHub for trending repositories in the 'machine-learning' topic
3. Check Hacker News for the most discussed AI/ML posts

Format as a clean summary with sections for each source. Include links.
Keep it under 500 words." --name "Weekly AI digest" --deliver telegram

code_examples.caption: GitHub repo watcher
code_examples.code: /cron add "every 6h" "Check the GitHub repository NousResearch/hermes-agent for new issues, PRs, or releases in the last 6 hours. Run: gh issue list --repo NousResearch/hermes-agent --state open --json number,title,author,createdAt --limit 10. If nothing new, respond with [SILENT]." --name "Repo watcher" --deliver discord

gotchas: Cron jobs run in fresh agent sessions with NO memory — prompts must be completely self-contained
Use [SILENT] in agent responses to suppress delivery for no-change monitoring results
The script parameter runs before the agent — its stdout becomes context for the agent's reasoning
Use execute_code for mechanical multi-tool data gathering, then delegate for reasoning-heavy analysis
Test with /cron run <job_id> before waiting for the schedule
Natural language like 'daily at 9am' is not supported — use cron expressions like '0 9 * * *'
no_agent=True mode is available for script-only jobs where the script already produces the desired output
full_text:
# Automate Anything with Cron

The daily briefing bot tutorial covers the basics. This guide goes further — five real-world automation patterns you can adapt for your own workflows.
